subject: ELA
title: Vocabulary: Context Clues & Word Roots
description: Using context, Greek/Latin roots, prefixes, and suffixes to unlock word meaning.

questions.q: The root 'bio' means life. What does BIOGRAPHY mean?
options: A written account of someone's life
questions.answer: A written account of someone's life
questions.explanation: Bio (life) + graphy (writing) = a written account of someone's life.

questions.q: The prefix 'pre-' means before. What does PREVIEW mean? (two words)
questions.explanation: Pre (before) + view (see) = to see something before its official release.

questions.q: 'The scientist made a significant discovery that changed medicine forever.' SIGNIFICANT most likely means...
questions.explanation: Context: changed medicine forever — something that has that effect is significant (important).

questions.q: The suffix '-tion' turns a verb into a noun. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: Adding -tion makes a noun: educate → education.

questions.q: The root 'aqua' means water. Give one example word using this root.
'Despite the turbulent storm, the pilot landed safely.' TURBULENT most likely means...
questions.explanation: Context: a dangerous storm a pilot had to land through = turbulent = rough/chaotic.

questions.q: Which word uses the prefix 'mis-' (meaning wrongly or badly)?
questions.explanation: Mis (wrongly) + take = mistake, meaning to take an action wrongly or incorrectly.

questions.q: The root 'port' means to carry. What does TRANSPORT mean? (two words)
questions.explanation: Trans (across) + port (carry) = transport = to carry something from one place to another.

questions.q: 'The scientist was meticulous about recording data, checking every number twice.' METICULOUS most likely means...
questions.explanation: Checking every number twice shows extreme care = meticulous.

questions.q: The root 'terr' means earth or land. What does TERRITORY mean?
questions.explanation: Terr (earth/land) + itory = territory = an area of land belonging to someone.
